title: cyphia sylvatica
intro: Cyphia sylvatica, also known as the woodland bugloss or yellow toadflax, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and potassium, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The herbal preparations of Cyphia sylvatica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and other conditions.
